resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def waysToBuyPensPencils(self, total: int, cost1: int, cost2: int) -> int:
        if cost1 > total or cost2 > total: return 1
        return total // cost1 + total // cost2 + (total // (cost1 + cost2) if cost1 + cost2 <= total else 0)
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses integer division to calculate the number of distinct ways to buy pens and pencils. It first checks if the cost of either pen or pencil exceeds the total amount of money, in which case there is only one way to buy 0 of each. Then it calculates the number of ways to buy pens and pencils separately, and finally adds the number of ways to buy both pens and pencils together if their combined cost does not exceed the total amount of money. This solution has a time complexity of O(1) and a space complexity of O(1), making it efficient for large inputs.
